cards.back: A queue is a linear data structure that follows the FIFO (First-In-First-Out) principle, where elements are added to the end and removed from the front.

cards.front: What is enqueue operation in a queue?
cards.back: Enqueue operation adds an element to the end of the queue.

cards.front: What is dequeue operation in a queue?
cards.back: Dequeue operation removes an element from the front of the queue.
A circular queue is a type of queue where the last element is connected to the first element, forming a circle.
A double-ended queue is a type of queue where elements can be added or removed from both ends.
